title: The power of music: How we became the conductors of the soundtracks of our lives
subhead: Although listening to music is a more widespread cultural practice than reading books or playing video games, it is less often a subject of debate. Yet the way we listen has evolved with technology, gradually shifting from a collective practice to an individual one, akin to a form of self-conditioning.

Al Jazeera says Israeli strike kills journalist in Gaza
subhead: The Israeli military issued a statement that cameraman Ahmed Wishah was killed in a 'precise strike' on Saturday, alleging that he was a Hamas militant. Wishah's brother and fellow Al Jazeera journalist Mohammed had also been killed in April by Israeli shelling.

title: France issues red heatwave alert on Sunday for third of country, bans public alcohol consumption at music festival
subhead: Météo-France announced the highest heatwave warning for 35 of France's departments for Sunday, including the Paris region. On Saturday, the government said that alcohol consumption in public spaces during the annual Fête de la Musique would be banned in departments under this alert.

title: French teachers find it a growing challenge to inspire a love of reading among students
subhead: French literature teachers have lamented a growing crisis within their classrooms. They argue that the traditional, formal nature of certain exercises and the list of required classics are failing to foster a desire to read among students.

title: Why is Henri Matisse such an influential artist?
subhead: As the Grand Palais in Paris hosts an exhibition dedicated to the French artist, Le Monde looks at what makes him one of the most celebrated names in painting.

2026 World Cup: Jérôme Meary, the Frenchman who became an essential football intermediary in the US
subhead: In just 15 years, the Haute-Savoie native has established himself as a key figure in Major League Soccer, the North American football league. Although nothing initially pointed him toward a career as an agent, a twist of fate changed everything.

title: In Poland, Jordan Bardella dreams of leading an alliance between Europe's right-wing and far-right parties
subhead: During a visit to Warsaw and the Belarusian border on June 18 and 19, the president of the Rassemblement National accelerated efforts to build an alliance with European conservatives and liberals – going so far as to distance himself from the Eurosceptic and sovereigntist stance Marine Le Pen has promoted for two decades.

title: Russia's shadow looms behind several sabotage and espionage attempts on French soil
subhead: At the beginning of June, counterintelligence agents arrested a man while he was filming a drone prototype in a factory near Toulouse. Other investigations are targeting individuals suspected of preparing acts of sabotage orchestrated from Russia.
